channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Pope Francis had peaceful night's rest at hospital following respiratory crisis, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis, 88, rested during a peaceful ninth night at Rome's Gemelli Hospital after a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, the Vatican said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis is conscious and receiving supplemental oxygen following a respiratory crisis
item.description: The Vatican says Pope Francis is still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen after doctors earlier said that the 88-year-old pope, who had part of one lung removed as a young man, was in critical condition.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel delays release of Palestinian prisoners, citing 'humiliating' handovers of hostages
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel says the release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners is delayed “until the release of the next hostages has been assured, and without the humiliating ceremonies” at handovers of Israeli captives in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, MOHAMMED JAHJOUH and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Meet Edan Alexander, the last living American hostage in Hamas captivity
item.description: Edan Alexander spent most of his life in Tenafly, New Jersey, before moving to Israel and enlisting in the IDF. On Oct. 7, 2023, he was taken hostage and is still being held in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in Berlin Holocaust Memorial stabbing wanted to kill Jews: Investigators
item.description: The suspect arrived in Germany in 2023 as an unaccompanied minor and successfully applied for asylum, investigators said. He lives in Leipzig.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu decries release of wrong body as a ceasefire violation. Hamas pledges to investigate
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowed revenge Friday for what he described as a “cruel and malicious violation” of the ceasefire agreement after authorities determined that a body released by Hamas was not an Israeli mother of two small boys, as the militant group had prom
item.dc:creator: AREEJ HAZBOUN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Suspect arrested after stabbing at Berlin's Holocaust memorial near US embassy
item.description: German police arrested a suspect in a stabbing attack at Berlin's Holocaust memorial on Friday. No motive has been given for the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis isn't out of danger but his condition isn't life-threatening, medical team says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis’ complex respiratory infection isn't life-threatening but he's not out of danger, his medical team said Friday, as the 88-year-old pontiff marked his first week in the hospital battling pneumonia in both lungs — along with bacterial, viral and fungal infections on top of his
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An encroaching desert threatens to swallow Mauritania’s homes and history
item.description: CHINGUETTI, Mauritania (AP) — For centuries, poets, scholars and theologians have flocked to Chinguetti, a trans-Saharan trading post home to more than a dozen libraries containing thousands of manuscripts. But it now stands on the brink of oblivion.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu calls for 'revenge' after Hamas returns wrong remains
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is vowing revenge after Hamas delivered the body of an anonymous woman instead of Shiri Bibas in violation of the current ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US not co-sponsoring UN resolution supporting Ukraine: Report
item.description: The U.S. is reportedly refusing to co-sponsor a draft United Nations resolution marking three years since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, signaling weakening U.S. support for Ukraine as President Trump is reaching out to Russia to end its war against the country.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump’s attempts to denigrate Zelenskyy have led to a surge in Ukrainian unity
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Days before the third anniversary of Russia's full-scale invasion, Ukrainians are as somber and tense as they were right before Moscow launched the war. Only now, they aren’t just worried about their longtime enemy.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US aid freeze puts HIV-positive orphans in Kenya at risk as medical supplies dwindle
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Two-year-old Evans was brought to the Nyumbani Children’s Home in Nairobi, Kenya a year ago, suffering from HIV and tuberculosis. With no family to care for him, Evans was referred to the orphanage by a health center after he stopped responding to medical treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS KOMU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel identifies remains of child hostages but says another body from Hamas was not their mother
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — The Israeli military said Friday it had positively identified the remains of two young hostages but another body released by Hamas under a ceasefire deal was not the boys’ mother as the militant group had promised.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, JOSEF FEDERMAN AND WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel's UN ambassador slams Hamas' 'evil and depraved' display of hostages' coffins
item.description: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk condemned Hamas' parading of the coffins containing Shiri, Ariel and Kfir Bibas and Oded Lifshitz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Terror attack rocks Israel; three buses reportedly explode
item.description: Israel officials have ordered all bus and train services halted while all vehicles are inspected for bombs following two bus explosions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fearing Trump will cross border militarily to pursue cartels, Mexico moves to reform constitution
item.description: Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum said she will propose constitutional reforms aimed at protecting the country’s sovereignty over concerns that the U.S. military could pursue drug cartels inside Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Philippine village offers cash for mosquitos amid dengue outbreak
item.description: To combat a dengue outbreak, a Philippine village is offering a bounty of one Philippines peso for every five mosquitoes residents turn in.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese warships make 'unusual' voyage near US ally Australia
item.description: As Chinese military ships voyage near Australia, Australian Minister for Defense Richard Marles described it as an "unusual event," and said that the country is surveilling the foreign ships.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump warns Zelenskyy to quickly negotiate war's end with Russia or risk not having a nation to lead
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday warned Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy that he “better move fast” to negotiate an end to Russia's invasion of Ukraine or risk not having a nation to lead.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, MIKE PESOLI and STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: "We found that more than 5 billion people—ie, more than 60% of the world's population—do not have access to safe, quality, and affordable medical oxygen services," the commission wrote.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's envoy arrives in Kyiv as US policy shifts toward Russia
item.description: Keith Kellogg, the U.S. special envoy for Ukraine and Russia, arrived in Kyiv on Wednesday for talks with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and military commanders, as the U.S. shifted its policy away from years of efforts to isolate Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Palestinians should be able to leave Gaza if they choose: Israel envoy
item.description: Hamas is speeding up the hostage exchange with Israel, announcing six people will be released Saturday instead of three.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Tourist in Turks and Caicos severely injured in shark attack
item.description: “While on what was supposed to be a relaxing vacation, my sister-in-law and her husband, my brother, lived what can only be described as a nightmare,” Al Chevarie,said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis has bilateral pneumonia but remains in good spirits, the Vatican says
item.description: Nevertheless the pope is in good spirits and is grateful for the prayers for his recovery, Vatican spokesman Matteo Bruni said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Delta plane crash: What we know about CRJ-900, aircraft that flipped over in Toronto
item.description: The CRJ-900 can seat either 70 or 76 people, but the layout and basic dimensions for the craft are nearly identical no matter the capacity.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Earth’s solid metal core may be soft on the outside: Study
item.description: Rather than being perfectly ball-shaped, the Earth's inner core may actually resemble peaks and valleys.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Food delivery driver helped stop suspect following deadly stabbing: officials
item.description: A 23-year-old with possible connections to the Islamic State group is in custody after a stabbing of six passersby that left a 14-year-old boy dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
